ranked_paths.path: Catalog-based (simulation) forecasting + negative-binomial / over-dispersed scoring to fix the MEASURED 7-day failure mode (count under-forecasting + Poisson N-test over-rejection during sequences), not the spatial shape every killed path attacked.
ranked_paths.why_it_survived: This is the only path that targets our ACTUAL dominant 7-day failure as recorded in the killed-H4 critique itself: 'gross count UNDER-forecasting during sequences (global n_forecast 1176 vs n_observed 3146, N-test pass 0.0).' Every killed H attacked spatial shape (count-neutral) and so could not touch this. The lever is peer-reviewed and operational, NOT hype: Werner et al. 2010 (negative-binomial total-count) + Savran et al. 2020 BSSA + Kagan 2017 GJI (Poisson grid tests over-reject during aftershock sequences) — already cited in our own csep.py module header. Our IGPE/N-test path is the gridded-Poisson half; the catalog-based path (pyCSEP N/M/S/PL on simulated catalogs) relaxes the independence assumption that makes our N-test fail at 0.0. Mechanism is mathematically real (our intensity integral mu*T+triggering is a Poisson MEAN; the realized count is over-dispersed), and it is leakage-free by construction (simulate forward from strictly-past conditioning). Survives the skeptics because it changes the dispersion/likelihood model, not the rate field — so it is orthogonal to 'no NPP beats ETAS' and to 'shape corrections are count-neutral.'
ranked_paths.concrete_next_experiment: E13-NB: Keep the base tiled-ETAS rate field UNCHANGED. (1) Add a catalog-based scoring branch: for each issue date, simulate K>=10,000 synthetic 7-day catalogs by thinning the fitted ETAS conditional intensity forward from strictly-past parents (the Omori/productivity machinery already vectorized in E11 gives this nearly free). (2) Route the simulated catalogs through pyCSEP's catalog-based number_test/spatial_test/pseudolikelihood_test (already a dependency) and ALSO fit a per-window negative-binomial total-count from the simulation spread (Werner 2010 alpha). (3) Re-run the 60-day multi-region back-analysis reporting BOTH the gridded-Poisson N-test (current, fails 0.0) and the catalog-based N-test, plus the NB-calibrated credible interval coverage. Pre-register: success = catalog-based N-test coverage rises from ~0 to >=0.9 AND the NB interval covers the observed sequence-window counts that the Poisson interval misses. This converts a chronic N-test FAIL into a passing, defensible consistency result and is a publishable honest-measurement contribution even if mean-rate IGPE does not move.
ranked_paths.expected_payoff_nats: Near-zero direct IGPE change (the rate field is unchanged), but fixes the N-test 0.0 -> ~0.9 and yields correctly-calibrated count uncertainty during sequences — the binding consistency failure. Secondary IGPE upside +0.00..+0.02 only if the dispersion-aware likelihood is also used to re-weight members in E12.

ranked_paths.path: Horizon-aware DEPLOYMENT of our own measured asset: ship the geodetic-neural BACKGROUND as a 14-30 day outlook product (where E11 measured robust +0.05..+0.10), and at 1-7d use the neural background ONLY as the ETAS mu(x,y) replacement (hybrid B) — gated to no-regression, never claimed as a 7d win.
ranked_paths.why_it_survived: This is grounded in OUR OWN leakage-free E11 numbers (7d {-0.16,-0.08,+0.14,+0.09}; 30d {+0.052,+0.019,+0.103}), not an imported literature claim, so it dodges the 'misstated evidence' kill that sank H2/H3/H9. The decisive E11 fact — the neural forecast is time-flat and scales linearly with horizon (18.0 at 7d, 77.3 at 30d, ratio 4.3~=30/7) — proves what it learned is a geodetic-conditioned BACKGROUND, which legitimately dominates the long-horizon integral. The HONEST framing is the survivor: do NOT route 7d windows to the neural (that was H2's killed gate); instead (a) ship a NEW 30d outlook tab where the asset is real and the literature agrees (GEAR1 global strain), and (b) at 7d only inject mu_neural as ETAS's background provider (the background= hook already exists in ETASForecaster), so triggering still dominates and there is no 7d regression by construction.
ranked_paths.concrete_next_experiment: E13b: Implement mu=mu_neural inside TiledForecaster via the existing background= injection point (the neural becomes a CovariateFieldProvider-backed background; smoothed null stays as cold-start floor). Then TWO leakage-free runs against base tiled ETAS with the E12 pre-registered gate: (i) at H=14 and H=30 — expected to PASS and ship the outlook tab; (ii) at H=1..7 — pre-register the no-regression target (mean IGPE >= -0.005, never worse than base). First confirm the gate prerequisite from the design doc: that mu_neural beats the smoothed null as a declustered long-horizon background in isolation. Report per-horizon IGPE crossover explicitly.
ranked_paths.expected_payoff_nats: +0.05..+0.10 at 14-30d (new product surface, measured, robust across E11 windows). At 1-7d: 0.000 by design (no-regression) — explicitly NOT a 7d gain over base ETAS.

ranked_paths.path: Temporally-adaptive convex re-weighting of the E12 ETAS-variant stack (dynamic w_t reweighted during sequences), instead of a single global weight vector — the lever the Taroni/Marzocchi 2023 template attributes its real gain to.
ranked_paths.why_it_survived: H8 was killed for proposing STEP as an orthogonality engine, but the kill itself surfaces the surviving mechanism: 'the paper attributes its small gain to temporal ADAPTIVITY (dynamic reweighting during sequences), not to decorrelated family residuals.' We have NOT tried this — E12 explicitly fits a SINGLE global weight vector ('no per-regime weights') and still cleared a small genuine +0.0087 driven by the long-memory V2. Making the weights time-adaptive (up-weight V1 short-memory at sequence onset, V2 long-memory in the late tail) is the documented source of the OEF-Italy operational gain and is consistent with finding F6 (no single model dominates all horizons). It survives the data-sparsity skeptic because it adds NO new members and NO per-cell parameters — only a single scalar memory/decay on the existing 2 free weights, fit from the rolling strictly-past log-score the E12 solver already computes.
ranked_paths.concrete_next_experiment: E12-adaptive: Extend EnsembleForecaster.fit_weights_from_history to an exponentially-time-weighted log-score (one new hyperparameter: the half-life tau over which past windows inform the current weight), anchor-shrunk to V0 exactly as E12. Re-run the E12 pre-registered multi-region 7d back-analysis with w_t(tau) vs the static-weight E12 vs base tiled ETAS. Negative control: shuffle the window order before fitting tau — adaptivity must beat the shuffle. Pre-register: ships only if it beats BOTH base AND static-E12 at 7d with the Rhoades-T CI positive, in >=60% of windows, no recent-quintile sign flip.
ranked_paths.expected_payoff_nats: +0.005..+0.015 at 7d over static E12 (the literature ceiling is +0.016 +/- 0.028; we already have +0.0087 static, so the adaptive increment is the small remaining headroom). Likely modest and possibly non-significant — a valid recorded outcome either way.

ranked_paths.path: Recent-quintile temporal-stratification guard + automated negative-control harness as a permanent scoring layer — institutionalize the over-fitting check (Koehler 2025 / F7) so any future lever's 7d gain is credited only if it survives the most-recent held-out windows and a shuffled-label control.
ranked_paths.why_it_survived: Not a rate-model change, so no killed-H critique applies — it is the measurement-rigor lever finding F7/F8 explicitly endorse and that our own E12 already half-implements (the shuffle control that exposed half of +0.0087 as a non-spatial artifact). It survives precisely because it makes EVERY other path on this menu honest and cheap to falsify: it is the thing that turns 'unprovable headline' into 'defensible-or-rejected.' Low effort, high leverage as infrastructure; it is the reason base ETAS keeps winning (it stops us shipping the artifacts the killed paths would have produced).
ranked_paths.concrete_next_experiment: Add to the back-analysis harness, as a standing gate applied to ALL comparison runs: (1) split the weekly windows into quintiles and report per-quintile IGPE — flag any lever whose sign flips on the most-recent quintile; (2) a built-in shuffled-label negative control (>=20 shuffles, report the max as the detection floor) auto-run alongside every igpe_vs_base_tiled channel; (3) emit a single boolean 'survives_stratification_and_control' that the E12-style ship rule consumes. Back-fill it onto E11 and E12 to confirm it reproduces the already-known artifacts.
ranked_paths.expected_payoff_nats: 0 direct (no rate change) — its payoff is preventing false-positive ships and quantifying the detection floor (~0.004 nats today), which is what makes ranks 1-3 trustworthy.

killed_paths.path: H1 time-varying per-tile b-positive + STAI incompleteness correction in the GR exceedance term
killed_paths.why_killed: Operational best practice (USGS OAF) holds b FIXED and corrects the RATE side; STAI is a low-Mc (M3-4.5) phenomenon entirely below our Mc=5.35 floor by ~day 1, so our M>=5.35 tail is not transiently incomplete. The on-target prospective test (Herrmann & Marzocchi 2024 BVAL) finds time-varying-b UNDERPERFORMS ETAS at operational coverage.

killed_paths.path: H2 regime/phase-GATED ensemble routing background windows to the geodetic-neural, active windows to ETAS
killed_paths.why_killed: Misreads EarthquakeNPP (no NPP advantage exists to route) and SCEC-2025 (marginal NPP gains are in immediate aftershocks — the inverse of the claimed split). Our E11 shows the neural's 7d positivity is a horizon-integral artifact of a time-flat background, not a class of background-dominated 7d windows; a gate cannot create per-window skill the component lacks, and adds leak-prone free parameters on the sparsity wall.

killed_paths.path: H3 multiplicative EEPAS-style strain-rate medium-term modulator on the ETAS background (lambda = lambda_ETAS * m(x))
killed_paths.why_killed: The cited NZ multiplicative hybrid is a LONG-TERM time-INVARIANT BACKGROUND construction (years-decades, retrospective), multiplying static spatial fields — it does NOT multiply across timescales. At 1-7d the background is a negligible fraction of the intensity integral (triggering dominates), so modulating it additively or multiplicatively cannot move 7d IGPE; additive-vs-multiplicative does not change WHICH term is modulated.

killed_paths.path: H4 GCMT nodal-plane anisotropic (fault-aligned) ETAS spatial kernel for M>=6 parents
killed_paths.why_killed: Cited IGPE gains come from distance-to-ACTUAL-rupture reconstructed retrospectively; static GCMT azimuth is the weakest proxy with a 90-degree plane ambiguity. At our 1.0-degree (~111 km) grid the M6-7 rupture elongation (~10-50 km) is sub-cell and washed out. Anisotropy is a count-neutral shape correction and cannot fix our dominant 7d failure, which is gross count UNDER-forecasting (1176 vs 3146, N-test 0.0).

killed_paths.path: H5 online Bayesian sequence-specific updating from regime priors (OAF generic-to-specific) during the active window
killed_paths.why_killed: Triple operating-point mismatch: (1) OAF cures a minutes-to-day cold-start gap we don't have (we recondition on the full catalog DAILY); (2) the specific transition is powered by abundant M2-3 aftershocks below our Mc=5.35 floor; (3) OAF's prospective gain is vs Reasenberg-Jones, framed as uncertainty reduction not mean IGPE, and we already measured the closest lever (E12 V2 empirical-Bayes shrinkage) at +0.0087 with half an artifact.

killed_paths.path: H6 catalog-derived quadtree multi-resolution grid to raise measured 7d IGPE
killed_paths.why_killed: Conflates S-test power with IGPE skill; the cited Khawaja/Serafini work EXPLICITLY excludes IGPE from the coarsening recommendation (use highest resolution for comparison tests). Empty cells do NOT dilute our IGPE (the sum runs only over observed events). Aggregating fine cells BLURS models where they differ, generically shrinking discriminative IGPE. Domain mismatch (5-yr time-independent M>=5.95 vs daily clustered 1-7d).

killed_paths.path: H7 MAGNET-style history-conditioned neural magnitude head replacing fixed-b GR
killed_paths.why_killed: MAGNET's +0.07 bits/eq is at Mc 1.6-2.5 with true location+time handed in (retrospective-by-construction); three mismatches with global M>=5 (Mc 5.35) at once, the paper itself reports only 'slight advantage at large earthquakes' and its info-gain DECREASES during the intense sequences that dominate M>=5 skill. The magnitude head perturbs only the GR tail Phi(M*), not the rate field that dominates M>=5 IGPE; our ETAS already passes the M-test (~93.8%).

killed_paths.path: H8 add a STEP-style instantaneous-Omori model as a stack member for orthogonality
killed_paths.why_killed: The exact cited template (Taroni/Marzocchi 2023) refutes the mechanism in its own words: the three families 'are all based on the same principles' and 'low diversity considerably limited the ensemble potential'; STEP is the WEAKEST member with near-zero weight. The +0.016 ceiling carries CI +/-0.028 (not significant at M>=4), and the real gain is from temporal adaptivity, not decorrelated families (which is why adaptivity survives as rank 3).

killed_paths.path: H9 inject a smoothed long-term/strain background PRIOR into ETAS inversion (harmonized-Europe style)
killed_paths.why_killed: Real evidence (Han, Mizrahi & Wiemer 2025 NHESS) is inseparable from a bespoke REGIONAL ESHM20 product in a regionally-calibrated Mc~4.6 1-day model whose headline is that the regional model beat global-parameter ETAS — so the lever does not transfer to a global tiled model where the analogous prior IS the smoothed null we already run as the mandatory floor. The sibling OEF background literature places the prior at 1-10 YEAR timescales. H9's actual delta is merely promoting the null from fallback to in-likelihood prior.

killed_paths.path: H10 add a K-function / spatial-CRPS clustering proper score to credit spatial skill separately from the artifact
killed_paths.why_killed: The K-function score has no prospective track record at our scale; Ripley's K against a single observed pattern of ~10-25 mostly-isolated global 7d M>=5 events has enormous sampling variance — it inherits the same low-count wall that weakens our S-test. It does not isolate the +0.0087 artifact (a rate/temporal-aggregation effect the shuffle control ALREADY catches) and would most likely return a noisy zero.

ranking.honest_bottom_line: Base tiled ETAS is at or very near the practical ceiling for the MEAN-RATE 1-7 day IGPE over global M>=5. Ten distinct adversarial hypotheses were killed, and the convergent reason is decisive: the proven prospective levers (score-weighted ETAS-family ensembles) cap at +0.016 +/- 0.028 nats with the CI crossing zero, our own best measured 7d result is +0.0087 with half of it a non-spatial artifact, NO neural/foundation TPP has beaten ETAS prospectively, and our own E11 confirms the geodetic-neural is horizon-dependent (loses at 7d, wins only at ~30d). Anyone promising a large 7d IGPE gain over base ETAS is selling retrospective/hype. HOWEVER, 'near the ceiling on mean-rate IGPE' is NOT the same as 'nothing left to fix.' Our actually-measured binding failure at 7d is not spatial shape (which every killed path attacked) — it is count UNDER-forecasting during sequences and a Poisson N-test that fails at 0.0 because the gridded-Poisson likelihood over-rejects over-dispersed sequence counts. The single most valuable, genuinely-new, well-evidenced move (rank 1) is therefore NOT another rate-shape lever but switching the SCORING/UNCERTAINTY model to catalog-based simulation + negative-binomial dispersion (Werner 2010; Savran 2020; Kagan 2017 — already cited in our own csep.py): it converts a chronic consistency FAIL into a passing, correctly-calibrated result and is a publishable honest-measurement contribution even with zero IGPE movement. Rank 2 monetizes our one real asset by deploying the geodetic-neural background as a NEW 14-30d outlook product (where +0.05..+0.10 is robustly measured) while guaranteeing no 7d regression. Ranks 3-4 are the small, honest, possibly-non-significant headroom that remains (temporal adaptivity of the existing stack, +0.005..+0.015) wrapped in a permanent stratification+negative-control guard. Net: pursue calibration/uncertainty correctness and a longer-horizon product surface rather than chasing a 7d mean-rate gain that the evidence says is below the detection floor. Relevant grounding files (absolute paths): D:/_Repos/_Web_Projects/CAOS_SEISMIC/src/caos_seismic/eval/csep.py (IGPE + the gridded-Poisson N-test that fails 0.0; catalog-based path noted as pyCSEP-owned in its header), D:/_Repos/_Web_Projects/CAOS_SEISMIC/src/caos_seismic/model/etas.py (the background= injection hook rank 2 uses, plus the vectorized expected_counts that makes catalog simulation cheap), D:/_Repos/_Web_Projects/CAOS_SEISMIC/wip/horizon-aware-and-hybrid-design.md (the E11 time-flat-background fact grounding rank 2), D:/_Repos/_Web_Projects/CAOS_SEISMIC/docs/experiments.md (E10-E12 results), D:/_Repos/_Web_Projects/CAOS_SEISMIC/docs/improvement-evidence.md (the cited prospective evidence base and the +0.016 ceiling).
